# image backend test fixtures

## `roomA_synth_pano.png`

A procedurally rendered equirectangular (panoramic) image of a shoebox room,
generated by our own spike renderer (`spike-image-geometry/render_synth_pano.py`).
It is **MIT-clean**: an original procedural render with no third-party image
assets, used purely as a deterministic input to lock the real HorizonNet
inference path (`tests/test_image_backend_golden.py`, `vision`-marked).

- Resolution: 1024 x 512 (equirectangular, RGB)
- Ground-truth room dimensions: W = 4.0 m, D = 3.0 m, H = 2.7 m
- Camera height: 1.6 m (room-centred)

The golden test runs the vendored HorizonNet on this pano and regression-locks
the (rough-tier) reconstruction output; the estimate is **not** install-grade
accuracy — see the test module docstring for the captured values and error.
